source_paper_title: Lower Complexity Bounds of First-Order Methods for Affinely Constrained Composite Nonconvex Problems
context:
Section 4 (Conclusion and Open Questions), page 25

First, for Algorithm Class 1 on solving Problem Class 1, can the lower bound \(O(\kappa([\bar A;A])L_f\Delta F_0\varepsilon^{-2})\) be achieved, i.e., is it tight?
title: Tightness of the \(\Omega(\kappa([\bar A;A])L_f\Delta F_0\varepsilon^{-2})\) oracle-complexity lower bound for Algorithm Class 1
latex:
Given integers \(d,n,\bar n\ge 1\), let \(A\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times d}\) and \(\bar A\in\mathbb{R}^{\bar n\times d}\) be matrices, and let \(b\in\mathbb{R}^n\), \(\bar b\in\mathbb{R}^{\bar n}\). Let \(f_0:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}\) be differentiable with \(L_f\)-Lipschitz gradient, i.e., \(\|\nabla f_0(x)-\nabla f_0(x')\|\le L_f\|x-x'\|\) for all \(x,x'\in\mathbb{R}^d\). Let \(\bar g:\mathbb{R}^{\bar n}\to\mathbb{R}\cup\{+\infty\}\) be a proper lower semicontinuous convex function such that \(\mathrm{relint}(\mathrm{dom}(\bar g))\neq\emptyset\) and \(\mathrm{dom}(\bar g)\) is not a singleton, and define \(g(x):=\bar g(\bar A x+\bar b)\) for \(x\in\mathbb{R}^d\). Consider the affinely constrained composite problem
\[
\min_{x\in\mathbb{R}^d}\;F_0(x):=f_0(x)+g(x)\quad\text{s.t.}\quad Ax+b=0.
\]
For a tolerance \(\varepsilon>0\), call \(x_\varepsilon\in\mathbb{R}^d\) an \(\varepsilon\)-stationary point if there exists \(\gamma\in\mathbb{R}^n\) such that
\[
\max\Big\{\mathrm{dist}\big(0,\nabla f_0(x_\varepsilon)+A^\top\gamma+\partial g(x_\varepsilon)\big),\;\|Ax_\varepsilon+b\|\Big\}\le\varepsilon,
\]
where \(\partial g(x_\varepsilon)\) is the convex subdifferential and \(\mathrm{dist}(0,S):=\inf\{\|s\|:s\in S\}\). Define the initial optimality gap \(\Delta F_0:=F_0(x^{(0)})-\inf_{x\in\mathbb{R}^d}F_0(x)\), where \(x^{(0)}\) is the algorithm initialization.

An Algorithm Class 1 method is any iterative method that, at each iteration \(t\ge 1\), may query an oracle that returns \(\nabla f_0(x)\), \(Ax\), \(A^\top z\) (for a chosen \(z\in\mathbb{R}^n\)), and \(\mathrm{prox}_{\eta g}(x)\) (for a chosen \(\eta>0\)), and then produces iterates according to the linear-span restriction stated in the paper’s Algorithm Class 1 definition.

Problem: Determine whether there exists an Algorithm Class 1 method and a universal constant \(C>0\) such that for every problem instance above and every \(\varepsilon\in(0,1)\), the method outputs an \(\varepsilon\)-stationary point using at most
\[
C\,\kappa([\bar A;A])\,L_f\,\Delta F_0\,\varepsilon^{-2}
\]
oracle calls (up to at most polylogarithmic factors in \(\varepsilon^{-1}\)), where \([\bar A;A]\in\mathbb{R}^{(\bar n+n)\times d}\) denotes the row-stacked matrix and
\[
\kappa([\bar A;A]) := \sqrt{\frac{\lambda_{\max}([\bar A;A][\bar A;A]^\top)}{\lambda^+_{\min}([\bar A;A][\bar A;A]^\top)}}
\]
with \(\lambda_{\max}(\cdot)\) the largest eigenvalue and \(\lambda^+_{\min}(\cdot)\) the smallest positive eigenvalue. Equivalently, decide whether the paper’s lower bound \(\Omega(\kappa([\bar A;A])L_f\Delta F_0\varepsilon^{-2})\) for Algorithm Class 1 is (nearly) tight.
open_problem_quote: First, for Algorithm Class 1 on solving Problem Class 1, can the lower bound \(O(\kappa([\bar A;A])L_f\Delta F_0\varepsilon^{-2})\) be achieved, i.e., is it tight?

verification.notice: This entry was generated from the paper text only. It has not been independently verified as still open. If you believe the question is solved or incorrectly stated, please contact the maintainers.
